# Festival Art & Books

> Festival Art & Books is a specialist antiquarian bookshop run by Mark Faith, one of the
> world's leading dealers in rare and collectible J.R.R. Tolkien books, including first
> editions of The Hobbit (1937) and The Lord of the Rings (1954–55), plus original
> Tolkien-inspired art. In business 25+ years.

## Tolkien Collector's Guide (full e-book, online)

- [The Tolkien Collector's Guide — hub](https://festivalartandbooks.com/collectors-guide): Mark Faith's complete free collector's guide, readable online chapter by chapter or downloadable as a PDF.
- [Introduction](https://festivalartandbooks.com/guides/collectors-guide-introduction): Who Mark Faith is, why he wrote the guide, and how to request a free copy.
- [Chapter 1: What to Buy](https://festivalartandbooks.com/guides/collectors-guide-what-to-buy): Which Tolkien titles and editions to collect, condition grading, signed books, dust-jacket restoration, and what actually holds value.
- [Chapter 2: Where to Buy](https://festivalartandbooks.com/guides/collectors-guide-where-to-buy): Specialist dealers vs auctions, negotiating, market fundamentals, and how to assess condition from photographs.
- [Chapter 3: Technicalities](https://festivalartandbooks.com/guides/collectors-guide-technicalities): Printing history, states and issues, proofs, and the Lord of the Rings impression chart compiled by Mark Faith.
- [Chapter 4: The Three Eras of Tolkien Books](https://festivalartandbooks.com/guides/collectors-guide-three-eras): Pre-hippie, collector-era, and post-Jackson collecting; deluxe editions and long-term value.

## Collecting Guides

- [Collecting Tolkien Books for Fun and Investment](https://festivalartandbooks.com/guides/collecting-tolkien-books-for-investment): Cornerstone guide by Mark Faith on how to collect and invest in Tolkien first editions, why condition is everything, how prices are set, and why ISBNs and issue points rarely matter.
- [The Ballantine Tolkien Paperbacks and Barbara Remington's Cover Art](https://festivalartandbooks.com/guides/ballantine-tolkien-paperbacks-barbara-remington): Why the rushed 1965 Ballantine paperbacks and their iconic Remington covers, which Tolkien disliked, helped propel his work to worldwide fame.

## Expert Q&A

- [Used & Rare Tolkien Books: Q&A](https://festivalartandbooks.com/used-tolkien-books): Answer-first guide from Mark Faith on first-edition values, dust jackets, restoration, states and what to collect.

## About the Dealer

- [About Mark Faith](https://festivalartandbooks.com/about): Background, credentials, and 25+ years in the rare Tolkien book trade.

## Current Inventory

- [Rare Tolkien Books & Collectables](https://festivalartandbooks.com/rare-tolkien-books-collectables): The current collection of rare and collectable Tolkien books, art and ephemera.
- [Current Catalogue](https://festivalartandbooks.com/books): Browse 88+ rare Tolkien books and collectables currently for sale — titles, photos, and prices synced from the live eBay shop.
- [eBay Shop](https://www.ebay.co.uk/str/festivalartandbooks): Current listings for rare Tolkien books and art.
- [AbeBooks Shop](https://www.abebooks.co.uk/festival-art-and-books-aberdyfi/51881760/sf): Current listings on AbeBooks.

## Free Resources

- [Resources hub](https://festivalartandbooks.com/resources): Index of guides, journal, events, media, art, and the photo gallery.
- [Free Tolkien Collector E-book](https://festivalartandbooks.com/ebook): Download the PDF or read the full guide online.
- [Archive Photo Gallery](https://festivalartandbooks.com/photo-gallery): Rare Tolkien photographs, press articles, and archive images from Mark Faith's collection.

## Optional

- [The Journal](https://festivalartandbooks.com/journal): Essays on Tolkien collecting, market commentary, and notable finds.
